definition: Parse each material formula into normalized elemental fractions and compute cosine similarity or L1-overlap between evaluation and training compositions. This axis asks whether property performance depends on chemical-composition overlap with known systems.
example_usage: python formula_composition_similarity.py --train train_materials.csv --eval eval_materials.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --formula-col formula

citations.title: Probing Out-of-Distribution Generalization in Machine Learning for Materials

evidence.summary: The paper separates compositional and structural origins of OOD failures and links poor performance to chemical dissimilarity.
The method uses compositional features from chemical formula and structural features from crystal structures to interpret bias.

limitations: The simple parser does not handle parentheses, hydrates, disorder, oxidation states, or fractional occupancy syntax.
Formula similarity ignores crystal structure and synthesis context.
Element fraction overlap may be too coarse for materials where oxidation state or local environment matters.
quality_gates: Normalize formulas consistently and record parser limitations.
Run a structure-based curve separately when structural data are available.
Check whether held-out elements or rare chemistries dominate low-similarity bins.
